question:
Problem A-1
Let \( V \) be the region in the cartesian plane consisting of all points \( (x, y) \) satisfying the simultaneous conditions
\[
|x| \leqslant y \leqslant|x|+3 \text { and } y \leqslant 4
\]

Find the centroid \( (\bar{x}, \bar{y}) \) of \( V \).
solution:
A-1.
Let \( T \) consist of the points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,3),(-1,4),(1,4) \) and let \( U \) be the set of points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,0),(-4,4),(4,4) \). Then \( T \) and \( V \) overlap only on boundary points and their union is \( U \). The centroids of \( T \) and \( U \) are \( (0,11 / 3) \) and \( (0,8 / 3) \), respectively. The areas of \( T, V \), and \( U \) are 1,15 , and 16 , respectively. Using weighted averages with the areas as weights, one has
\[
1 \cdot 0+15 \bar{x}=16 \cdot 0, \quad 1 \cdot \frac{11}{3}+15 \bar{y}=16 \cdot \frac{8}{3} .
\]

It follows that \( \bar{x}=0, \bar{y}=13 / 5 \).

descriptive_long.question:
Problem A-1
Let \( regionv \) be the region in the cartesian plane consisting of all points \( (coordinatex, coordinatey) \) satisfying the simultaneous conditions
\[
|coordinatex| \leqslant coordinatey \leqslant|coordinatex|+3 \text { and } coordinatey \leqslant 4
\]

Find the centroid \( (centroidx, centroidy) \) of \( regionv \).
descriptive_long.solution:
A-1.
Let \( trianglet \) consist of the points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,3),(-1,4),(1,4) \) and let \( triangleu \) be the set of points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,0),(-4,4),(4,4) \). Then \( trianglet \) and \( regionv \) overlap only on boundary points and their union is \( triangleu \). The centroids of \( trianglet \) and \( triangleu \) are \( (0,11 / 3) \) and \( (0,8 / 3) \), respectively. The areas of \( trianglet, regionv \), and \( triangleu \) are 1,15 , and 16 , respectively. Using weighted averages with the areas as weights, one has
\[
1 \cdot 0+15 centroidx=16 \cdot 0, \quad 1 \cdot \frac{11}{3}+15 centroidy=16 \cdot \frac{8}{3} .
\]

It follows that \( centroidx=0, centroidy=13 / 5 \).

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Problem A-1
Let \( harboring \) be the region in the cartesian plane consisting of all points \( (mapleleaf, monarchic) \) satisfying the simultaneous conditions
\[
|mapleleaf| \leqslant monarchic \leqslant|mapleleaf|+3 \text { and } monarchic \leqslant 4
\]

Find the centroid \( (windcastle, stormcloud) \) of \( harboring \).
descriptive_long_confusing.solution:
A-1.
Let \( lighthouse \) consist of the points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,3),(-1,4),(1,4) \) and let \( snowdrifts \) be the set of points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,0),(-4,4),(4,4) \). Then \( lighthouse \) and \( harboring \) overlap only on boundary points and their union is \( snowdrifts \). The centroids of \( lighthouse \) and \( snowdrifts \) are \( (0,11 / 3) \) and \( (0,8 / 3) \), respectively. The areas of \( lighthouse, harboring \), and \( snowdrifts \) are 1,15 , and 16 , respectively. Using weighted averages with the areas as weights, one has
\[
1 \cdot 0+15 windcastle=16 \cdot 0, \quad 1 \cdot \frac{11}{3}+15 stormcloud=16 \cdot \frac{8}{3} .
\]

It follows that \( windcastle=0, stormcloud=13 / 5 \).

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Problem:
<<<
Problem A-1
Let \( emptiness \) be the region in the cartesian plane consisting of all points \( (constantval, horizontal) \) satisfying the simultaneous conditions
\[
|constantval| \leqslant horizontal \leqslant|constantval|+3 \text { and } horizontal \leqslant 4
\]

Find the centroid \( (boundaryval, boundaryvertical) \) of \( emptiness \).
>>>
descriptive_long_misleading.solution:
Solution:
<<<
A-1.
Let \( linearity \) consist of the points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,3),(-1,4),(1,4) \) and let \( singularity \) be the set of points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,0),(-4,4),(4,4) \). Then \( linearity \) and \( emptiness \) overlap only on boundary points and their union is \( singularity \). The centroids of \( linearity \) and \( singularity \) are \( (0,11 / 3) \) and \( (0,8 / 3) \), respectively. The areas of \( linearity, emptiness \), and \( singularity \) are 1,15 , and 16 , respectively. Using weighted averages with the areas as weights, one has
\[
1 \cdot 0+15 boundaryval=16 \cdot 0, \quad 1 \cdot \frac{11}{3}+15 boundaryvertical=16 \cdot \frac{8}{3} .
\]

It follows that \( boundaryval=0, boundaryvertical=13 / 5 \).
>>>

garbled_string.question:
Let \( pqlkdrtm \) be the region in the cartesian plane consisting of all points \( (qzxwvtnp, hjgrksla) \) satisfying the simultaneous conditions
\[
|qzxwvtnp| \leqslant hjgrksla \leqslant|qzxwvtnp|+3 \text { and } hjgrksla \leqslant 4
\]

Find the centroid \( (\bar{mbczlqsj}, \bar{nxfjegop}) \) of \( pqlkdrtm \).
garbled_string.solution:
A-1.
Let \( akmsidpq \) consist of the points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,3),(-1,4),(1,4) \) and let \( jvrcehzw \) be the set of points inside or on the triangle with vertices at \( (0,0),(-4,4),(4,4) \). Then \( akmsidpq \) and \( pqlkdrtm \) overlap only on boundary points and their union is \( jvrcehzw \). The centroids of \( akmsidpq \) and \( jvrcehzw \) are \( (0,11 / 3) \) and \( (0,8 / 3) \), respectively. The areas of \( akmsidpq, pqlkdrtm \), and \( jvrcehzw \) are 1,15 , and 16 , respectively. Using weighted averages with the areas as weights, one has
\[
1 \cdot 0+15 \bar{mbczlqsj}=16 \cdot 0, \quad 1 \cdot \frac{11}{3}+15 \bar{nxfjegop}=16 \cdot \frac{8}{3} .
\]

It follows that \( \bar{mbczlqsj}=0, \bar{nxfjegop}=13 / 5 \).

kernel_variant.question:
Let
\[
V=\{(x,y)\in\mathbb R^{2}\mid |x|\le y\le |x|+5\text{ and }y\le 9\}.
\]
Find the coordinates \((\bar x,\bar y)\) of the centroid of the planar region $V$.
kernel_variant.solution:
1.  Symmetry.
   The defining inequalities are unchanged when x is replaced by -x, so V is symmetric about the y-axis.  Hence x=0.

2.  View V as a difference of two triangles.
   The lines y=|x| and y=9 intersect at (-9,9) and (9,9), while y=|x|+5 and y=9 meet at (-4,9) and (4,9).  Define
     U = \Delta ((0,0),(-9,9),(9,9)),
     T = \Delta ((0,5),(-4,9),(4,9)).
   One checks that T and V share only boundary points and that U=T\cup V.  Thus V=U\setminus T.

3.  Areas of U and T.
   Both triangles have horizontal bases and vertical heights:
     [U]=\frac{1}{2}\cdot 18\cdot 9=81,   [T]=\frac{1}{2}\cdot 8\cdot 4=16.
   Hence [V]=81-16=65.

4.  Centroids of U and T.
   The centroid of a triangle is the arithmetic mean of its vertices:
     C_U=(0,6),   C_T=(0,23/3).

5.  Area-weighted addition.
   Since U = T\sqcup V (up to boundary),
     [U]C_U = [T]C_T + [V](x,y).
   From the x-coordinate:  81\cdot 0 =16\cdot 0+65\cdot x \Rightarrow  x=0.
   From the y-coordinate:
     81\cdot 6 =16\cdot (23/3)+65\cdot y
     \Rightarrow 486=368/3+65y
     \Rightarrow 65y=1090/3
     \Rightarrow y=218/39.

Therefore the centroid of V is
   (x,y) = (0, 218/39).

core_steps: Note symmetry about the y-axis to deduce \bar{x}=0
View V as the difference U \ T where U and T are two triangles that share only boundary
Find the areas of triangles U and T with the usual ½·base·height formula
Find the centroids of U and T by averaging their three vertex coordinates
Use the area-weighted (additive) centroid formula to obtain \bar{y}

slot1.description: vertical offset between the two slanted boundaries |x| ≤ y ≤ |x| + c

slot2.description: height of the horizontal cap y ≤ h
